title: [Your Project] Architecture Deep Dive
description: Understand the architectural patterns that make [Your Project] work - [pattern 1], [pattern 2], and [pattern 3]

steps.title: [Pattern Name 1]
steps.description: [Replace with: Explain the first core architectural pattern. Example: 'EditorState is immutable - never modified directly. Every update clones the state, applies changes, then replaces it atomically. This enables time-travel debugging and prevents race conditions. Implemented in EditorState.ts.']

items.comment: Replace with files implementing this pattern

steps.title: [Pattern Name 2]
steps.description: [Replace with: Explain the second architectural pattern. Example: 'Updates use double-buffering like graphics rendering. Updates.ts clones EditorState, batches all mutations, then Reconciler.ts diffs and applies minimal DOM changes. This ensures consistency and performance.']

items.comment: Replace with files implementing this pattern

steps.title: [Extensibility Pattern]
steps.description: [Replace with: Explain how the system can be extended. Example: 'Commands enable decoupled communication. Plugins dispatch commands, handlers respond by priority. Commands.ts defines the pattern. This allows extensions without tight coupling.']

steps.title: Patterns in Practice
steps.description: [Replace with: Show how patterns work together in practice. Example: 'React layer turns architectural concepts into composable components. Composer provides editor context, plugins hook into lifecycle. Each plugin is independent but composed: HistoryPlugin, RichTextPlugin, ListPlugin.']
